title: Has anyone watched Revival of Evangelion
display_title: Has anyone watched Revival of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Has anyone watched Revival of Evangelion?             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Tue Aug 17, 2010 8:48 pm If so, Where can one get this version of Death and End of Evangelion? How do you think both films work together as one, especially in terms of length? (If I am not mistaken, both amount to 140 minutes) And is the live action sequence substituted for the one where we see a live action sequence that takes place 15 years into the future and where Shinji doesn't exist?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Aug 17, 2010 9:25 pm Renewal: The Feature Films is kind of Revival , just Renewal-quality footage/audio and both films (Death and EoE, no Rebirth) are on separate films instead of seamless. I think the actual Revival was a "in-Japanese theaters only" thing (instead of an eventual VHS/DVD release), but I could be remembering wrong. The scrapped live-action sequence that has the seiyuu for Misato/Rei/Asuka is included as a bonus feature on the Renewal DVD for EoE, I believe.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Tue Aug 17, 2010 10:04 pm Thanks for the info. (Sigh) Too bad it's rather expensive and that it has no subtitles. I would love to have a good copy of EoE on DVD.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Wed Aug 18, 2010 4:37 am You know, I have that DVD. Was that DVD released after the Manga Ent. version? If so, is it better?

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Webster » Wed Aug 18, 2010 6:36 pm berto wrote: You know, I have that DVD. Was that DVD released after the Manga Ent. version? If so, is it better?

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Wed Aug 18, 2010 6:40 pm Dr. Webster wrote: I think most people here would agree that anything is better than the Manga release of EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Wed Aug 18, 2010 7:30 pm shinji_ryoji_89 wrote: Thanks for the info. (Sigh) Too bad it's rather expensive and that it has no subtitles. I would love to have a good copy of EoE on DVD.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Wed Aug 18, 2010 9:39 pm Clover wrote: You aren't missing much. EoE looks like shit. It's always going to look like shit until a Blu-Ray upscale. The amount of video noise, frame shake, and film imperfections in the final DVD release is absolutely disgusting. I can't watch the movie on anything but an old standard def T.V. anymore without wanting to throw up. I can't imagine what the original prints looked like during the theatrical run, but they must have been somewhat better than this.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Wed Aug 18, 2010 10:24 pm shinji_ryoji_89 wrote: I've heard the Japanese "renewal" edition of it looks great.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Wed Aug 18, 2010 11:36 pm They need to make a BlueRay of this already. I know Manga Ent. has the rights so there wouldn't be a US ver. but a Jp ver. without subs is better than none.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Wed Aug 18, 2010 11:46 pm berto wrote: They need to make a BlueRay of this already. I know Manga Ent. has the rights so there wouldn't be a US ver. but a Jp ver. without subs is better than none.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Thu Aug 19, 2010 6:56 pm Clover wrote: I'm watching the renewal version. It's pig disgusting unless it's on a standard T.V. Plus, there are weird blue shadows at random times that are obviously not part of the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by visionfactory » Sat Aug 21, 2010 3:39 pm I downloaded something that is supposed to be the Revival of Evangelion, here is a screenshot (it's so much better than the Manga release of EoE, I think it has extra scenes and now the Evangelions screams, I think they don't scream in the original version of the movie, please correct me if I'm wrong)

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Tue Aug 24, 2010 6:19 pm visionfactory wrote: I downloaded something that is supposed to be the Revival of Evangelion, here is a screenshot (it's so much better than the Manga release of EoE, I think it has extra scenes and now the Evangelions screams, I think they don't scream in the original version of the movie, please correct me if I'm wrong)

[]:             Quote Post by visionfactory » Wed Aug 25, 2010 4:51 pm ^ Thanks a lot for claryfing that!

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Thu Aug 26, 2010 6:14 pm If someone's interested, here's the same frame straight from the DVD of the DTS Collector's Edition (R2 PAL, 2009). (click on the image for a lossless PNG)

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Sep 02, 2010 4:22 am How did you take your screenshoot Username? This is my screenshoot of my loved EoE's 02 (from the Renewal DVD): I've left the aspect ratio uncorrected and I've used a PC scale YUV>RGB conversion and the image lost that "old"-like effect that you've mentioned while retaining more details compared to the Zentharim's rip. EDIT: Also why do you keep saying that Zentharim upscaled it? It's 704x480 anamorphic, he simply removed the borders of the movie, he didn't change the resolution, the visual difference is mainly the work of some filtering and probably of the AVC in-loop filter which soften the video by itself in an adaptive way (and while AVC is of course better than MPEG-2 a 7:1 ratio is probably not possible to achieve without some visible loss).

(If I am not mistaken, both amount to 140 minutes) And is the live action sequence substituted for the one where we see a live action sequence that takes place 15 years i
